_meta.description: Exercise library. Each exercise is a standalone atom referenced by day templates. Edit here to change how an exercise is described everywhere it appears.

ankle-circles.name: Ankle Circles
ankle-circles.howTo: Seated, foot lifted off the floor. Draw large, slow circles with your toes — 10 clockwise then 10 anticlockwise. Both feet.

wall-mobilisation.name: Wall Mobilisation (Knee-to-Wall)
wall-mobilisation.howTo: Kneeling lunge facing wall. Drive your knee forward toward the wall, keeping the heel flat on the floor.
cues: Heel stays FLAT on the floor — always
Gradually move foot closer each week as range improves

calf-stretch.name: Calf Stretch
calf-stretch.howTo: Facing wall, one foot back with a straight leg. Lean forward until you feel the calf stretch.

glute-bridge.name: Glute Bridge
glute-bridge.howTo: Lie on back, feet flat hip-width. Drive heels down and lift hips.
cues: Squeeze RIGHT glute deliberately at top

bridge-march.name: Bridge with March
bridge-march.howTo: Bridge up fully. At the top, lift the LEFT foot 2 inches off the floor and alternate feet.
cues: Do not let hips drop or rotate

single-leg-bridge.name: Single-Leg Bridge
single-leg-bridge.howTo: Bridge up on the RIGHT foot only, LEFT leg extended straight up. Drive through the grounded heel.

clamshell.name: Side-Lying Clamshell
clamshell.howTo: Lie on side, hips stacked, knees bent. Open the RIGHT (top) knee upward.

clamshell-band.name: Clamshell + Band
clamshell-band.howTo: Same as clamshell, with a resistance band placed just above the knees. Press the RIGHT knee out against the band.

bird-dog.name: Bird-Dog
bird-dog.howTo: On all fours, flat back, core engaged. Extend the RIGHT arm forward and LEFT leg back.

bird-dog-hold.name: Bird-Dog + Hold
bird-dog-hold.howTo: Same as Bird-Dog, but hold the extension for 5 sec and add a small forward reach at the end.

hip-abduction.name: Hip Abduction
hip-abduction.howTo: Side-lying, RIGHT (top) leg straight. Lift it 12–18 inches, squeeze the glute at the top.
cues: Add ankle weight only if pain-free

box-squat.name: Box Squat to Chair
box-squat.howTo: Stand in front of a sturdy chair. Lower hips until barely touching the seat.
cues: Never a full unsupported air squat

wall-sit.name: Wall Sit
wall-sit.howTo: Back flat against the wall, slide down until knees are bent (not 90° initially). Engage quads throughout.

calf-raise.name: Standing Calf Raise
calf-raise.howTo: Hold the wall lightly for balance. Raise heels slowly off the floor, controlled slow descent.

scap-retraction.name: Scapular Retraction
scap-retraction.howTo: Seated or standing. Pull shoulder blades together and DOWN simultaneously. Hold 5 sec, release slowly.

wall-angel.name: Wall Angel
wall-angel.howTo: Back flat on wall, arms in goalpost. Slowly raise arms overhead then back down, keeping full contact with the wall.
cues: Keep full arm contact with wall throughout

band-row.name: Band Row
band-row.howTo: Band at chest height, anchored ahead. Pull elbows back, squeezing shoulder blades together.

face-pull.name: Face Pull
face-pull.howTo: Band at eye level. Pull handles to ears with external rotation, elbows high throughout.
cues: Skip or reduce during cycle flare

prone-ytw.name: Prone Y/T/W
prone-ytw.howTo: Face down on mat. Lift arms into Y (overhead), T (to sides), then W (bent elbows). Tiny, fully controlled lift.

single-arm-row.name: Single-Arm Row
single-arm-row.howTo: Using a 3 lb dumbbell, row one arm at a time, squeezing the shoulder blade back.

cat-cow.name: Cat-Cow
cat-cow.howTo: On hands and knees. Inhale: spine sags, head lifts (Cow). Exhale: spine arches, chin tucks (Cat).

puppy-pose.name: Puppy Pose
puppy-pose.howTo: From all fours, walk hands forward while hips stay over knees. Let chest melt toward floor.

supine-twist.name: Supine Twist
supine-twist.howTo: Lie on back, knees to chest, drop both knees to one side. Opposite shoulder stays grounded.

crescent-lunge.name: Crescent Lunge
crescent-lunge.howTo: Gentle lunge with back knee lifted, hips square, slight backbend. Props as needed.

savasana.name: Modified Savasana
savasana.howTo: Lie flat, bolster or folded blanket under BENT knees, arms relaxed. Breathe deeply and slowly.
cues: Props are essential for lumbar support

tree-pose.name: Tree Pose (wall support)
tree-pose.howTo: Stand near a wall for support. Place one foot to the opposite inner calf or thigh (not on the knee).
cues: Never foot on the knee joint

hip-flexor-kneeling.name: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
hip-flexor-kneeling.howTo: Half-kneeling: one knee on the floor, other foot flat forward. Tuck pelvis under gently and shift weight forward until you feel the stretch in the front of the back hip.
cues: Tuck pelvis — don't arch the low back

pigeon-prep.name: Supine Pigeon (Figure-Four Stretch)
pigeon-prep.howTo: Lie on your back. Cross one ankle over the opposite thigh just above the knee. Flex that foot. Draw the bottom leg toward your chest until you feel the stretch in the outer hip.
cues: Flex the crossed foot — protects the knee
Right side will likely be tighter — be patient

childs-pose.name: Child's Pose
childs-pose.howTo: Kneel and sit back toward your heels, walking hands forward on the mat. Forehead rests down. Knees can be wide for more comfort.
cues: Block under forehead if neck is tight

seated-forward-fold.name: Seated Forward Fold
seated-forward-fold.howTo: Sit with legs extended. Hinge forward from the hips — lead with the chest, not the forehead. Strap or towel around the feet if needed.
cues: Bend knees if hamstrings are very tight

mountain-pose.name: Mountain Pose (Tadasana)
mountain-pose.howTo: Stand tall, feet hip-width. Press all four corners of each foot into the floor. Thighs lightly engaged, arms relaxed at sides, shoulders rolled back and down.
cues: Lift through the crown of the head
This is active standing — not just standing

forward-fold.name: Standing Forward Fold (Uttanasana)
forward-fold.howTo: From standing, hinge at the hips and fold forward. Hands to the floor, a block, or your shins. Bend the knees as much as you need.
cues: Hinge at hips — not the waist
Shake the head yes and no to release the neck

half-lift.name: Half Lift (Ardha Uttanasana)
half-lift.howTo: From forward fold, lift halfway. Fingertips to shins or thighs. Flat back, gaze forward and slightly down.
cues: Long spine — not a rounded back
This is a position, not a stretch — hold it

low-lunge.name: Low Lunge (Anjaneyasana)
low-lunge.howTo: From all fours or downward dog, step one foot forward between your hands. Lower the back knee to the mat (pad it). Sink the hips gently.
cues: Pad under back knee if needed
Hips square — don't let them open to one side

downward-dog.name: Downward Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana)
downward-dog.howTo: From all fours, tuck toes and lift hips up and back. Press firmly through all fingers. Pedal the heels gently.
cues: Bend knees generously if hamstrings are tight
Left heel: don't force it to the floor
Spread fingers wide, no collapsing into wrists

warrior-1.name: Warrior I (Modified)
warrior-1.howTo: From low lunge, lift the torso and raise arms overhead. Back foot turns to 45°. Use the wall for balance as needed.
cues: Wall available — use it without shame
Left foot: don't force heel flat
Square the hips forward, even if the back foot protests

warrior-2.name: Warrior II (Modified)
warrior-2.howTo: From lunge, open hips to the side. Arms extend parallel to the floor, gaze over the front hand. Back foot turns parallel to the mat edge.
cues: Front knee tracks over 2nd toe — always
Arms strong but shoulders relaxed away from ears
Sink into the front hip without collapsing

pec-stretch.name: Doorway Chest Stretch
pec-stretch.howTo: Stand in a doorway, arm at 90°, forearm resting on the frame. Gently rotate your body away until you feel the stretch across the chest. Keep the shoulder blade engaged down.
cues: Cycle flare: skip or do the gentlest version only

lat-stretch.name: Lat Stretch (Side Reach)
lat-stretch.howTo: Stand or sit tall. Reach one arm overhead and lean gently to the opposite side. Or hold a door frame and lean your bodyweight away from it.
cues: Long side body — not a crunch
Keep both hips grounded if seated
